seoTitle: How to Create a Custom Dashboard in Airtable
metaDescription: Build an interactive dashboard in Airtable's Interface Designer with charts, metric summaries, and filters, then publish it for your team.
intro: Airtable's Interface Designer lets you turn raw base data into a visual dashboard with charts, number summaries, and grid elements without writing any code. This guide walks through building a dashboard from a blank canvas, connecting your data source, and publishing it with the right viewer permissions.

steps.heading: Open the Interface Designer
steps.body: From your Airtable workspace, launch the Interface Designer hub to start building a new dashboard.

steps.heading: Start a blank dashboard
steps.body: Create a new interface page and select the blank dashboard template as your starting point.

steps.heading: Connect your data source
steps.body: Link the base tables that contain the data you want to visualize on the dashboard.

steps.heading: Add visual components
steps.body: Drag and drop chart components, number summaries, and grid elements onto the canvas to build out your layout.

steps.heading: Configure widget settings
steps.body: Adjust each widget's filters, grouping, and colors so it displays the specific data points you want.

steps.heading: Publish with the right permissions
steps.body: Rearrange the layout for clarity, then click Publish and set viewer access permissions before sharing.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Airtable plan to use Interface Designer?
faq.answer: Interface Designer is available on Airtable's plans, though some advanced widgets and viewer limits may depend on your specific plan tier.

faq.question: Can viewers interact with the dashboard, or is it static?
faq.answer: You can add interactive filters that let viewers sort and adjust what data the dashboard shows without editing the underlying base.

faq.question: What kinds of charts can I add to an Airtable dashboard?
faq.answer: You can add number summaries and various chart components tied directly to your linked base tables, configurable through each widget's settings.

faq.question: Can I limit who sees the published dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, when you publish you configure viewer access permissions to control who can open the interface.
